notes: Use PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% in launch options
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes:
This game is cross platform but does not have cross platform multiplayer and there are never any Linux players online.

The game Launches fine in proton but crashes half way through the performance test. Performance is also very poor, Graphics settings on minimum yields visually  poor frame rates with what seems to be consistent micro pauses every second.
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Proton version: Disable esync, and set "audio quality" to low in the sound tab options in game, otherwise the audio bugs out and game crashes. Game is playable and multiplayer is finally an option, considering the Linux player base is nearly non existent.
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes:
The native version doesn't support multiplayer with windows, so running this with proton is required for online multiplayer.
To get the work to not crash in the tutorial, you need to set the sound settings to low.
To get the game not to crash in the online matches, you have to disable esync.
After these workarounds, everything works fine.
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Game runs great as long as Esync is disabled and the audio quality in-game is set to LOW. Setting the audio to medium or high will cause looping/glitching audio and instability (crashes).
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Works almost perfect, but as others have noted sound is buggy. Sadly, setting the sound quality to low didn't fix it for me. And playing this game with no sound does not make sense.. :(

notes: The game used to run (Although unstable requiring tweaks) and I put tons of hours in to the multiplayer, but it does no longer. It refuses to starting giving a "Cannot find supported hardware rendering device" error. Most likely a GPU driver issue.

title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Game works native on Linux. On Proton with DXVK it will freeze during the loading screen e.g. when starting a campaign. Disabling Esync didn't help, but the game worked before for me.
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: What Works: Cross Platform Multiplayer With Windows Users (YAY). Graphics on High. Good Performance. What does not work: Stable Audio. Sound starts looping on a specific audio track. Tested with Low/Medium and High Sound Settings. Happens on all. Second is the game will crash on the end game loading screen on both Local AI  skirmish and Online Multiplayer games. Otherwise works great and is a big step up guys!
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Besides disabling ESYNC and turning the sound settings to low, I had to match the bit depth of my sound configuration (16bit in my case) with a key in the configuration_system.lua file called "soundvoices" (value = 16 instead of 32 as it was set by the game). The game is running in Windows10 compatibility mode, using winecfg to set it, since it had lowered the glitchy sound problem while i was tinkering the solution (might be unrelated). The game is perfectly playable now, sometimes some sounds do not play for a split second, but i don't know if it's due to the low bit depth i had set or to something else.
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Game refuses to launch with error "failed to find a supported hardware rendering device". Tried forcing all the available specific versions of proton, with no change. Tried all the proton runtime options. Using PROTON_USE_WINED3D caused a different error "failed to set a working mode for the rendering device" Tried with and without disabling esync, as suggested by other posters. Native version is flawless, but I want multiplayer, my friends are on Windows and the pool of players for matchmaking is tiny on linux
title: Company of Heroes 2

notes: Game runs fine when disabling esync (match will not start otherwise). There is a mayor problem with audio which makes this game unplayable for me: After ~5 minutes into a game, the audio buffer will loop for the rest of the game (last second or so).
